wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the ark-agentic framework. Cover installation via uv package manager, initial setup with environment variables, and basic usage examples. Include quick start examples showing how to create a simple agent, register tools, and run conversations. Document the CLI commands for initializing new projects, adding agents, and running in different modes (mock, interactive). Provide step-by-step tutorials for common scenarios like insurance withdrawal and securities queries. Include troubleshooting for common setup issues and links to more detailed documentation sections.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tools Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the tools development system. Document the AgentTool base class architecture, tool lifecycle management, and execution patterns. Explain tool registration mechanisms, parameter validation, result formatting, and error handling strategies. Cover tool development best practices including memory integration, A2UI rendering capabilities, and state management. Include practical examples of creating custom tools for different domains, implementing proper error handling, and optimizing tool performance. Document the tool executor patterns, concurrent execution strategies, and tool composition techniques. Address tool debugging, testing methodologies, and integration with the broader agent ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Insurance Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the insurance agent implementation. Detail the insurance agent's purpose, capabilities, and workflow patterns. Document the agent configuration in agent.json, including A2UI settings and behavioral parameters. Explain the insurance-specific tools and skills available, particularly withdrawal processing and policy management. Cover the A2UI integration for dynamic UI rendering in insurance contexts. Include practical examples of insurance agent usage, common use cases like policy withdrawals and claims processing, and integration patterns with the broader agent ecosystem. Document the guard mechanisms and safety checks specific to insurance operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the agent architecture in ark-agentic. Explain the ReAct execution loop pattern, AgentRunner lifecycle, and how agents orchestrate tools and skills. Document the message flow between system, user, assistant, and tool roles. Cover the execution cycle including planning, tool selection, execution, and result processing. Include practical examples showing how agents handle complex workflows, manage state, and coordinate multiple operations. Address the separation of concerns between different agent types (insurance, securities) and their shared architectural patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for session management system. Document the SessionManager class architecture, session lifecycle operations (create, load, delete, sync), and state persistence mechanisms. Explain the dual-layer persistence system using TranscriptManager for message storage and SessionStore for metadata. Cover synchronous vs asynchronous operations, session state management, token usage tracking, and message operations including injection and clearing. Include practical examples of session creation patterns, state updates, and proper cleanup procedures. Document configuration options for session retention, automatic cleanup, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Chat API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the core chat endpoint. Document the POST /chat endpoint with detailed request/response schemas including ChatRequest and ChatResponse models. Cover all request parameters: message, agent_id, user_id, session_id, context, history, run_options, and streaming options. Document response formats for both streaming (SSE) and non-streaming responses. Include authentication headers (x-ark-user-id, x-ark-session-id, x-ark-message-id, x-ark-trace-id), idempotency handling, and session management. Provide detailed examples of request/response payloads, error handling scenarios, and streaming event formats. Document the protocol types (agui, internal, enterprise, alone) and their specific SSE event structures.

wiki_catalogs.name: AG-UI Streaming Protocol
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AG-UI streaming protocol. Document all 17 event types defined in the AG-UI standard including run lifecycle events (run_started, run_finished, run_error), step events (step_started, step_finished), text message events (text_message_start, text_message_content, text_message_end), tool call events (tool_call_start, tool_call_args, tool_call_end, tool_call_result), state events (state_snapshot, state_delta), messages snapshot, thinking message events, and custom/raw events. Explain the event bus architecture, how runner callbacks map to AG-UI events, and the output formatting pipeline. Include practical examples of implementing custom streaming responses, handling event sequences, and managing real-time communication between backend and frontend systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Studio authentication system. Document the login workflow, JWT token management, protected route implementation, and user session handling. Explain the authentication flow from login to protected resource access, including error handling and security considerations. Cover user role-based access controls, permission systems, and logout procedures. Include practical examples of implementing authentication guards, managing user state, and handling authentication errors in the frontend. Document the backend authentication endpoints and security measures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Concepts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the core concepts of the ark-agentic framework. Explain the fundamental architecture including ReAct execution loop, AgentRunner lifecycle, and the relationship between tools, skills, and agents. Document the core data types, message flow, and execution patterns. Cover the tool system architecture, skill loading mechanisms, and how components interact. Include conceptual diagrams and practical examples showing how these concepts work together in practice. Address the separation of concerns between different architectural layers and how they contribute to the overall system design.

wiki_catalogs.name: Skills Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the skills development system. Document the Skill base class architecture, skill configuration format, and skill execution patterns. Explain the skill loading modes (full, dynamic, semantic), skill matching algorithms, and skill resolution strategies. Cover skill development best practices including skill composition, parameter validation, result formatting, and performance optimization. Include practical examples of creating domain-specific skills, implementing skill hierarchies, and integrating skills into agent workflows. Document the skill matcher algorithms, skill selection criteria, and skill execution contexts. Address skill testing, evaluation methodologies, and debugging techniques for skill development.

wiki_catalogs.name: Securities Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the securities agent implementation. Document the securities agent's role in financial data analysis and portfolio management. Explain the agent configuration, A2UI presets, and template rendering system. Cover the securities-specific tools including account overview, holdings analysis, profit calculations, and security detail retrieval. Detail the schema validation system and data transformation processes. Include practical examples of securities agent usage for asset analysis, profit tracking, and market data queries. Document the mock data system and testing patterns specific to securities operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the tool system architecture. Explain the AgentTool base class, tool registration mechanisms, and execution patterns. Document the tool lifecycle from discovery to execution, including parameter validation, result handling, and error management. Cover specialized tools like memory tools and state management. Include practical examples of tool implementation, registration, and invocation. Address tool composition patterns, parallel execution capabilities, and integration with the broader agent ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Memory Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the memory systems architecture. Document the three-tier memory approach: Session JSONL for conversation history, MEMORY.md for persistent knowledge, and System Prompt injection for context. Explain the Dream memory distillation process, knowledge extraction algorithms, and user profile management. Cover memory compaction strategies, context compression techniques, and knowledge retention policies. Include practical examples of memory writing patterns, extraction workflows, and optimization techniques. Document configuration options for memory limits, compression thresholds, and dream scheduling. Address memory cleanup procedures and performance considerations for large-scale deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Studio Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the Studio management interface. Document all administrative endpoints for agent management, skill administration, tool configuration, session monitoring, and memory inspection. Cover CRUD operations for agents, skills, and tools including creation, modification, deletion, and listing endpoints. Document session management APIs for viewing active sessions, user conversations, and session metadata. Include memory inspection endpoints for accessing agent memories and conversation histories. Provide authentication requirements, request/response schemas, and practical examples for each endpoint group. Document the relationship between Studio API and the core agent runtime.

wiki_catalogs.name: A2UI Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the A2UI component system. Document the complete component architecture including card, button, form, chart, and other UI components. Explain the component rendering pipeline, template system, and frontend integration. Cover the A2UI block system, component composition patterns, and state management. Include practical examples of creating custom A2UI components, implementing component templates, and handling component interactions. Document the JavaScript renderer, component lifecycle, and real-time updates. Address component styling, theming support, and responsive design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the Studio dashboard interface. Document the main dashboard layout, navigation structure, and overview statistics display. Explain the agent listing interface, system health indicators, recent activity feeds, and quick access controls. Cover the responsive design implementation, navigation patterns, and user experience flows. Include practical guides for navigating the dashboard, understanding system status, and accessing different management sections. Document the layout components and how they integrate with the overall Studio architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agents
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the agent system architecture and implementation. Document the different agent types including insurance, securities, and meta-builder agents. Explain agent configuration, initialization patterns, and how agents integrate with tools and skills. Cover the agent lifecycle, state management, and session handling. Include practical examples of creating custom agents, configuring agent-specific behaviors, and extending existing agents. Document the agent.json configuration format and how to customize agent behavior through configuration files.

wiki_catalogs.name: Registry and Loader Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the registry and loader systems that manage tools and skills. Document the centralized registry architecture, registration patterns, and discovery mechanisms. Explain the skill loader implementation including loading modes, caching strategies, and dynamic loading capabilities. Cover the registry configuration, tool registration processes, skill registration workflows, and lookup mechanisms. Include practical examples of extending the registry system, implementing custom loaders, and managing large-scale tool/skill catalogs. Document the registry performance optimizations, memory management, and concurrent access patterns. Address registry maintenance, cleanup procedures, and troubleshooting common registry issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Meta-Builder Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the meta-builder agent system. Explain the meta-builder pattern for creating agents programmatically and dynamically. Document the factory pattern implementation and how meta-builders orchestrate agent creation and management. Cover the meta-builder tools for managing agents, skills, and tools within the system. Detail the meta-builder guide documentation and best practices for agent composition. Include practical examples of using meta-builders to create specialized agents, configure agent hierarchies, and implement complex multi-agent workflows. Document the relationship between meta-builders and the standard agent lifecycle.

wiki_catalogs.name: Skill System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the skill system architecture. Explain skill loading mechanisms, metadata-based discovery, and automatic vs manual invocation patterns. Document the skill matching algorithm, dependency resolution, and conflict handling. Cover skill packaging, distribution, and version management. Include examples of skill implementation, metadata configuration, and integration patterns. Address skill composition, conditional loading, and performance optimization strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for the API system. Document the authentication mechanisms used in both the core chat API and Studio management API. Cover header-based authentication methods (x-ark-user-id, x-ark-session-id, x-ark-message-id, x-ark-trace-id) and their roles in the system. Document the Studio login API and session-based authentication flow. Include environment variable configuration for security settings, API key management, and access control patterns. Provide examples of authenticated requests and common authentication scenarios. Document security best practices, token handling, and authorization policies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Streaming Integration Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for streaming integration patterns. Document how to integrate streaming responses with FastAPI endpoints, handle Server-Sent Events (SSE), and manage real-time communication. Explain the integration between backend streaming events and frontend UI updates, including the thinking tag parser for processing AI reasoning streams. Cover practical examples of implementing chat interfaces, real-time dashboards, and interactive applications. Include best practices for error handling, connection management, and performance optimization in streaming scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Management Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Agent Management interface. Document the agent detail view, configuration panels, and management workflows. Explain the agent lifecycle management, including creation, modification, activation, and deletion processes. Cover the agent configuration options, parameter settings, and integration with the backend services. Include practical guides for configuring agents, monitoring agent performance, and troubleshooting common issues. Document the agent service APIs and how the frontend interacts with agent management functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tools and Skills
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the tools and skills system. Document the AgentTool base class, tool registration patterns, and execution lifecycle. Explain the skill system architecture including skill loading modes (full, dynamic, semantic), skill matching algorithms, and skill execution patterns. Cover tool development best practices, parameter validation, result formatting, and error handling. Include practical examples of creating custom tools and skills, registering them with the system, and integrating them into agent workflows. Document the skill configuration format and how to optimize skill performance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Data Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the core data type definitions used throughout the ark-agentic framework. Document all enums (MessageRole, ToolResultType, ToolLoopAction), dataclasses (ToolCall, AgentToolResult, AgentMessage, SkillMetadata, SessionEntry), and Pydantic models. Explain the relationships between types, validation rules, and serialization patterns. Include practical examples of type usage in different contexts, conversion utilities, and best practices for working with these foundational types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Skill Management Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the Skill Management interface. Document the skill visualization components, skill configuration panels, and testing interfaces. Explain the skill lifecycle from creation to deployment, including skill metadata management, parameter configuration, and performance monitoring. Cover the skill testing workflows, evaluation interfaces, and debugging tools. Include practical guides for developing new skills, configuring skill parameters, and validating skill functionality. Document the skill service integration and how skills are managed within the agent ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management and Memory
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for session management and memory systems. Document the SessionManager architecture, session lifecycle, and state persistence. Explain the three-tier memory system (Session JSONL, MEMORY.md, System Prompt injection) and the Dream memory distillation process. Cover context compression with LLM summarization, memory flush operations, and user profile management. Include practical examples of session state manipulation, memory writing patterns, and optimizing memory usage. Document configuration options for memory retention, compression thresholds, and dream scheduling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Management Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Tool Management interface. Document the tool configuration panels, tool registry management, and tool lifecycle operations. Explain the tool development workflow, parameter configuration, and integration patterns with agents. Cover the tool testing interfaces, validation processes, and performance monitoring capabilities. Include practical guides for creating custom tools, configuring tool parameters, and integrating tools into agent workflows. Document the tool service APIs and how tools are registered and managed within the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Streaming and UI Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the streaming and UI framework. Document the AG-UI protocol with all 17 event types, streaming architecture, and multi-protocol output support. Explain the A2UI component system including card, button, form, and chart components. Cover the streaming event bus, output formatter, and event assembly patterns. Include practical examples of implementing custom streaming responses, creating rich UI components, and handling real-time user interactions. Document the web UI integration, component rendering pipeline, and frontend-backend communication patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session and Memory Monitoring
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for the Session and Memory monitoring interfaces. Document the session tracking components, memory visualization tools, and real-time monitoring capabilities. Explain the session lifecycle management, conversation history viewing, and memory state inspection. Cover the memory analysis tools, data persistence monitoring, and performance metrics display. Include practical guides for monitoring agent conversations, analyzing memory usage patterns, and troubleshooting session-related issues. Document the session and memory APIs and how the frontend provides real-time insights into system operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the FastAPI endpoints. Document all HTTP endpoints including POST /chat with request/response schemas, authentication methods, and streaming protocols. Cover the SSE event formats for different protocol types (agui, internal, enterprise, alone). Include detailed parameter specifications, response codes, error handling, and rate limiting information. Document the Studio API endpoints for agent management, skill administration, and tool configuration. Provide Postman collection examples and client implementation guidelines for different programming languages.

wiki_catalogs.name: CLI and Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for CLI utilities and command-line tools. Document the main ark-agentic CLI commands including project initialization, agent scaffolding, and development utilities. Cover the template system for generating project skeletons and agent configurations. Include practical examples of using CLI for rapid prototyping, testing, and deployment preparation. Document utility scripts for data generation, publishing, and maintenance tasks. Provide troubleshooting guides for common CLI issues and best practices for CLI-driven development workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Studio Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Studio management interface. Document the frontend React application architecture, component structure, and user workflows. Cover the agent management dashboard, skill visualization, tool configuration interface, and session monitoring capabilities. Explain the authentication system, user roles, and access controls. Include practical guides for using the Studio interface to develop and deploy agents, monitor agent performance, and manage system resources. Document the backend API integration and real-time updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Configuration and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for configuration management and deployment strategies. Document environment variable configuration, dependency management with uv, and production deployment options. Cover Docker containerization, Kubernetes deployment patterns, and cloud-native deployment strategies. Include configuration examples for different environments (development, staging, production), security considerations for API keys and credentials, and monitoring setup. Document CI/CD integration patterns and automated deployment pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing and Development
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for testing strategies and development workflows. Document the testing architecture including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end tests. Cover test fixtures, mocking strategies, and test data management. Include guidelines for writing effective tests for agents, tools, and skills. Document the development workflow, debugging techniques, and performance profiling. Provide examples of testing streaming responses, memory operations, and complex agent interactions. Include continuous integration setup and quality assurance practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting content and frequently asked questions. Document common issues with installation, configuration, and runtime operation. Cover LLM provider connectivity problems, tool execution failures, and memory system issues. Include debugging strategies for streaming problems, session corruption, and performance bottlenecks. Provide solutions for environment-specific issues, dependency conflicts, and platform compatibility problems. Document error codes, log analysis techniques, and escalation procedures. Include FAQ sections addressing the most common user questions and edge cases.

wiki_overview.content:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of the ark-agentic project, I can now generate a detailed overview:

<blog>

# Ark-Agentic: Lightweight ReAct Agent Framework

## Project Introduction

Ark-Agentic is a lightweight ReAct (Reasoning and Acting) agent framework designed for building intelligent conversational agents with advanced capabilities including tool execution, memory management, and streaming responses. The framework provides a unified API service that can host multiple specialized agents for different domains such as insurance and securities management.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Modular Agent Architecture**: Support multiple specialized agents (insurance, securities) within a single framework
- **ReAct Pattern Implementation**: Enable agents to think, plan, and execute actions through tool usage
- **Streaming Capabilities**: Provide real-time streaming responses with structured event handling
- **Memory Management**: Offer optional persistent memory systems for context retention
- **Dual-Domain Support**: Seamlessly handle both insurance business processes and securities financial data

### Target Audience

- Enterprise developers building conversational AI solutions
- Financial institutions requiring automated customer service
- Insurance companies needing intelligent policy processing
- Securities firms requiring automated client advisory services

## Technical Architecture

The framework follows a layered architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[FastAPI Application] --> B[Agent Registry]
    B --> C[Insurance Agent]
    B --> D[Securities Agent]
    B --> E[Meta Builder Agent]
    
    C --> F[Agent Runner]
    D --> F
    E --> F
    
    F --> G[LLM Caller]
    F --> H[Tool Executor]
    F --> I[Session Manager]
    F --> J[Memory Manager]
    
    G --> K[LangChain LLM]
    H --> L[Tool Registry]
    I --> M[History Compaction]
    J --> N[Memory Storage]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several key layers:

1. **API Layer**: FastAPI-based REST API with streaming support
2. **Agent Layer**: Specialized agents for different domains
3. **Execution Layer**: Core ReAct execution engine
4. **Integration Layer**: Tool execution and external service integration
5. **Persistence Layer**: Memory and session management

### Design Patterns

- **Factory Pattern**: Used for creating different types of agents
- **Registry Pattern**: Centralized agent management and discovery
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable tool implementations
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven streaming responses
- **Template Method**: Standardized agent lifecycle management

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The application entry point is defined in `src/ark_agentic/app.py`, which creates a unified FastAPI application that hosts multiple agents:

- **Primary API**: `/api/chat` endpoint for agent interactions
- **Health Check**: `/health` endpoint for service monitoring
- **Static Pages**: HTML interfaces for insurance and securities domains
- **Studio Interface**: Optional management console accessible via `/studio`

### Core Modules

#### Agent Registry (`src/ark_agentic/core/registry.py`)
Centralized management of agent instances with simple registration and lookup operations.

#### Agent Runner (`src/ark_agentic/core/runner.py`)
Implements the core ReAct execution loop with support for:
- Tool execution orchestration
- Session management and history compaction
- Memory integration
- Streaming response handling

#### Tool System (`src/ark_agentic/core/tools/base.py`)
Provides a standardized interface for all agent tools with JSON schema validation and LangChain compatibility.

### Configuration Approach

Environment-based configuration through `.env-sample` with comprehensive settings for:
- LLM provider selection and authentication
- Memory and session persistence
- Domain-specific service endpoints
- Feature toggles (Studio, memory, thinking tags)

### External Dependencies

Key dependencies managed through `pyproject.toml`:
- **FastAPI/Uvicorn**: Web framework and ASGI server
- **LangChain**: LLM integration and chain management
- **NumPy/Pandas**: Data processing and manipulation
- **AkShare**: Financial data access
- **PyYAML**: Configuration parsing

### Integration Points

The framework integrates with external services through:
- **Insurance Data Services**: Policy queries and customer information
- **Securities APIs**: Account overviews, holdings, and market data
- **LLM Providers**: OpenAI, PA-SX, and PA-JT integrations
- **Authentication Systems**: Optional Studio login and authorization

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[API Router] --> B[Agent Registry]
    B --> C[Agent Runner]
    C --> D[LLM Provider]
    C --> E[Tool Registry]
    C --> F[Session Manager]
    C --> G[Memory Manager]
    
    E --> H[Domain Tools]
    H --> I[Insurance Tools]
    H --> J[Securities Tools]
    
    I --> K[Policy Query]
    I --> L[Customer Info]
    I --> M[Withdrawal Execution]
    
    J --> N[Account Overview]
    J --> O[Holdings Analysis]
    J --> P[Profit Calculation]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

1. **Multi-Agent Architecture**: Support for multiple specialized agents within a single service
2. **ReAct Execution Engine**: Intelligent reasoning and tool execution workflow
3. **Streaming Responses**: Real-time event-driven response delivery
4. **Memory Management**: Optional persistent memory with automatic summarization
5. **Session Handling**: Automatic session creation and history management
6. **A2UI Rendering**: Advanced UI composition system for dynamic interfaces
7. **Studio Management**: Optional web-based administration interface

### Implementation Highlights

#### Insurance Agent (`src/ark_agentic/agents/insurance/agent.py`)
Specialized for insurance business processes including:
- Policy information retrieval
- Customer data validation
- Withdrawal plan processing
- Risk assessment integration

#### Securities Agent (`src/ark_agentic/agents/securities/agent.py`)
Financial domain expertise with:
- Asset portfolio analysis
- Market data integration
- Profit calculation and ranking
- Security detail retrieval

#### A2UI System (`src/ark_agentic/core/a2ui/`)
Advanced UI composition with:
- Template-based rendering
- Dynamic component generation
- Transform system for data processing
- Theme and styling support

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialized
    Initialized --> Ready: Agent Registration
    Ready --> Processing: User Request
    Processing --> ToolExecution: Tool Call
    ToolExecution --> Processing: Tool Result
    Processing --> Completed: Final Response
    Completed --> Ready: New Request
    Processing --> Error: Exception
    Error --> Ready: Recovery
```

The framework provides a robust foundation for building enterprise-grade conversational AI systems with clear separation of concerns, extensible architecture, and comprehensive tooling for development and deployment.

**Sources:**
- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml)
- [app.py](src/ark_agentic/app.py)
- [registry.py](src/ark_agentic/core/registry.py)
- [chat.py](src/ark_agentic/api/chat.py)
- [agent.py](src/ark_agentic/agents/insurance/agent.py)
- [runner.py](src/ark_agentic/core/runner.py)
- [studio/__init__.py](src/ark_agentic/studio/__init__.py)
- [renderer.py](src/ark_agentic/core/a2ui/renderer.py)
- [base.py](src/ark_agentic/core/tools/base.py)
- [.env-sample](.env-sample)
- [main.py](src/ark_agentic/cli/main.py)
- [agent.py](src/ark_agentic/agents/securities/agent.py)
- [composer.py](src/ark_agentic/core/a2ui/composer.py)
- [conftest.py](tests/conftest.py)

</blog>
